title: andropogon villosus var. dactyloides
intro: Andropogon villosus var. dactyloides, also known as buffalo grass, is a type of perennial grass native to South Africa and the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to aid in the healing of wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The medicinal properties of buffalo grass are also believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of buffalo grass can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
